_comment: bypassPermissions ONLY ships because the PreToolUse hook suite below enforces hard blocks in EVERY permission mode — the bypass is earned by the hooks. If you remove or weaken the hooks, remove bypassPermissions too (or flip defaultMode to acceptEdits; every guard still enforces). The permissions.deny rules are a SECOND, native layer: deny hard-blocks even under bypassPermissions and even if the hooks were removed (docs/SECURITY.md). The deny list enumerates real env files instead of a .env.* wildcard so .env.example — the one env file MEANT to be read — stays readable; exotic variants are still caught by the hook. Distilled from todoclaw, in production 2026-06-23 → 2026-07-03.
_local_note: No PATH override ships here — a machine-specific PATH in a template breaks other machines. If node/npm don't resolve in Claude's shell, put an env.PATH in .claude/settings.local.json (gitignored). The PreToolUse command wraps python3 in a command -v guard so a machine WITHOUT python3 fails CLOSED (blocks all tools) instead of silently running unguarded.
